2606.07721.title: Automatic Extraction of Structured Information from Brain MRI Reports Using an Open-Weight Large Language Model
2606.07721.paper_summary: Objectives: Automatic data extraction from free-text radiology reports enables large-scale research, but few studies assessed the performance of large language models (LLMs) on Dutch neuroradiology reports. Methods: We analyzed 947 brain MRI reports from a tertiary memory clinic (2016-2021), authored by consultant neuroradiologists. Trained medical students annotated thirty variables; 100 reports were double-annotated to assess inter-rater reliability. We evaluated the performance of the open-weight LLM LLaMA 3.1 using different languages (Dutch vs. English translation) and few-shot prompting with different example selection strategies. Performance was evaluated using balanced accuracy for categorical variables, accuracy and mean absolute error for counts, and text similarity for free-text. Metrics were computed across 10 random splits of the 947 reports. Results: LLaMA 3.1 demonstrated high zero-shot performance for visual rating scores (mean [95%-CI]): Medial Temporal Atrophy: 90% [77-100%] on the left and 96% [94-99%] on the right, Global Cortical Atrophy: 87% [83-91%], and Fazekas: 94% [93-96%]. Microbleed mentions were detected with 93% accuracy [92-95%] and infarct mentions with 82% [80-84%]. Text similarity for lesion location reached 0.95 [0.95-0.96]. Performance was lower for numerical variables: 80% [78-82%] for the number of microbleeds and 66% [63-68%] for infarcts. English translation yielded comparable results. Few-shot prompting improved performance for numerical variables, achieving 92% [90-93%] for microbleeds and 81% [77-85%] for infarcts using structural similarity-based selection. Conclusion: LLaMA 3.1 shows strong potential for extracting data from Dutch neuroradiology reports. Few-shot prompting enhances performance for numerical variables, whereas challenges remain for location-specific variables.
2606.07721.paper_summary_zh: 目標：從自由文本放射學報告中自動提取數據使大規模研究成為可能，但很少有研究評估大型語言模型（LLMs）在荷蘭神經放射學報告上的表現。<lb/>方法：我們分析了來自一家三級記憶診所（2016-2021年）的947份腦部MRI報告，這些報告由顧問神經放射科醫生撰寫。<lb/>受過訓練的醫學生標註了三十個變量；為了評估評分者之間的一致性，100份報告進行了雙重標註。<lb/>我們使用不同語言（荷蘭語與英語翻譯）和不同示例選擇策略的少量提示來評估開放權重LLM LLaMA 3.1的表現。<lb/>對於類別變量，我們使用平衡準確率評估表現，對於計數則使用準確率和平均絕對誤差，對於自由文本則使用文本相似度。<lb/>指標是在947份報告的10個隨機拆分中計算的。<lb/>結果：LLaMA 3.1在視覺評分分數上顯示出高零樣本性能（平均[95%-CI]）：內側顳葉萎縮：左側90% [77-100%]，右側96% [94-99%]，全球皮質萎縮：87% [83-91%]，Fazekas：94% [93-96%]。<lb/>微出血提及的檢測準確率為93% [92-95%]，而梗塞提及的準確率為82% [80-84%]。<lb/>病變位置的文本相似度達到0.95 [0.95-0.96]。<lb/>數值變量的表現較低：微出血的數量為80% [78-82%]，而梗塞為66% [63-68%]。<lb/>英語翻譯的結果相當。<lb/>少量提示改善了數值變量的表現，微出血達到92% [90-93%]，梗塞達到81% [77-85%]，使用基於結構相似性的選擇。<lb/>結論：LLaMA 3.1顯示出從荷蘭神經放射學報告中提取數據的強大潛力。<lb/>少量提示增強了數值變量的表現，而位置特定變量仍然面臨挑戰。
2606.07721.author: Kaouther Mouheb et.al.
2606.07721.authors: Kaouther Mouheb, Amos Pomp, Antoine Manenti, Romy de Haan, Farog Faghir, Joy Martens, Harro Seelaar, Francesco Mattace-Raso, Meike W. Vernooij, Frank J. Wolters, Stefan Klein, Esther E. Bron
